reviews.review_description: to the creators of this game i would like to give a warm thank you to i cannot begin to describe to you what it is like being in the mind of an idhd individual firmly rooted in quantum physics and seeing reality through a lens much like what you see in this game my life truly is what you are playing in this game i could not have put it any better what it's like to be inside this mind but you managed to make a way for people to see through my eyes thank you

This experience effectively tells the stories of individuals living with mental health conditions and diagnoses. The narrations and visuals are incredibly well done and mess around with the player’s perception of space. I like this aspect of VR games a lot. The storytelling may feel a little repetitive after a while, but overall still a good experience.

reviews.review_title: A Game that Hit Close to Home
reviews.review_description:
I’ll start with the cons, as I think most people read reviews for those. The game required you to turn your back way too much in my opinion, I wish the game positioned the story in front of the player character, or they could add a visual indicator like an arrow gesturing for the player to physically turn towards the story animation.

Now for the pros. I’m someone diagnosed with Adhd, so that makes me biased toward the premise. But, seeing something that relates so heavily come to life was equal parts validating and jarring. The decision to make the controllers trail behind you when you move your hands was genius, because while it added some excitement for the neurotypical viewers, I think it was also a great fidgeting method, as I would actively move the controllers to have a fun physical activity to keep me focused on the story. 

They also have multiple stories in this game, which both makes it worth the price tag in my opinion and it shows how the condition isn’t a one size fits all experience. 

If you or someone you know has Adhd, I highly recommend this game, although make sure to have tissues because it gets heavy at some points.

Deep, meaningful, engaging, interesting, and incredibly creative. There are so many nice elements crammed into this well told story it makes other experiences feel lazy and short of ideas. In particular I really liked the mixed reality stuff. The fake shadows were a stroke of genius. I didn't love the visual design of the main narrative animations, but this is a top arts purchase for your quest.
Im so excited to be able to share this with others. And now share with my family

Impulse is more than just a game — it’s a vital, emotionally intelligent mixed reality experience that dares to tackle the complexities of mental health and neurodivergence. By placing the player inside the mind of someone living with ADHD, Impulse creates a powerful, immersive space where distractions, emotions, and coping mechanisms are not only visualised but deeply felt.

As someone with ADHD I'm glad this experience is being represented and hope there's opportunities to showcase the sheer diversity of what ADHD can be. 


This experience is a meaningful step toward destigmatising mental health struggles, and it's fantastic to see it's ability to not only win accolades on the creative industries but see it brought into health and wellbeing contests as an educational tool. 

Ace work, Anagram and everyone involved!

developer_response.body:
We set out to create something honest and embodied, that might speak not only to those with lived experience but also to those around them who are learning to understand. It’s encouraging to hear that intention came through with you, @STicho ! 

ADHD is incredibly diverse in how it shows up, and we hope Impulse adds to the growing conversation around its complexity. We're so glad it resonated with you and hope it sparks meaningful dialogue in and around your circles too.

reviews.review_description: I loved Goliath, and absolutely loved this - Anagram keep pushing out the best interactive documentaries that feel both grand and personal. Impulse is a joy.

developer_response.body: Each work is a few years in the making, so to know you connected with both means a lot to us @hanney!

reviews.review_title: I feel seen for the first time
reviews.review_description: As someone who has dealt with ADHD their whole life, this is far and away the best depiction of the challenges of living with ADHD that I have seen in any medium. I haven't had such a visceral, emotional reaction to something in years. To the creators of this - thank you.

developer_response.body: Hi @NotLawReview Thank you for sharing this—it really means a lot to hear the experience connected with you in such a real way. Capturing the moments and challenges of living with ADHD in an honest, relatable way was so important to us, and knowing it resonated with you makes all the effort worth it.

reviews.review_description: Unless you are really interested in what goes on in the mind of a person/s with ADHD this interactive thingy is incredibly dull. When it starts it lulls you into thinking you actually might be playing a game, there are some game like mechanics. But you suddenly realise the games are unwinnable and that's where all interactivity stops, that's about 10% in, the rest is just different people with ADHD explaining what goes on in their heads. Thats it, that's all you get, a 20 min "this is what ADHD is like" preachy mixed reality mess. This app tries terribly to make a point but it's seriously lacking any emotional punch. So all in all if you don't like expensive propaganda then stay away from this. You can learn a whole lot more about ADHD by grabbing a free pamphlet at you doctors ( free ) or simply searching on the net ( also free ).

Didn't realize this would be a full mixed reality experience... so well done!! Another wow experience for me and I'm 6 months into using my quest 3. I do wish there was an option for HD as the animated portion of the film feels low rez. Loved the feel of this film though and that I was part of the experience. One of the best mr experiences I've had.  Keep them coming.... please!! 🔥🔥🔥❤️❤️❤️✌️

developer_response.body: Hey @amerDad4Life, thank you for the awesome feedback! 🙌 We’re so glad you had an incredible time with the mixed reality experience, especially being 6 months into using your Quest 3. That’s saying something! 🎮✨🔥

reviews.review_description:
Very disappointing. As someone with ADD and the father of a child with a extreme ADHD, I found this experience to be a fictional representation of a very real condition. They cherry picked extreme outliers for case examples of people with ADHD that amounts to sensationalism. 

Contrary to the premise of Impulse, most people with ADHD aren’t sociopaths constantly risking their lives, causing mayhem, and utterly unable to follow rules and norms of the world around them. They just need constant stimulation in order to suppress the need to seek it out at the expense of what they should be doing at any given moment.  People with ADHD can live very normal lives and actually have advantages in certain skill sets due to extreme focus in areas of high interest.  Medication helps, but discipline and maturity can help one overcome the disadvantages and harness the advantages of a fast brain for a normative slow brain world.  

Bottom line: ADHD brains are just wired differently. Maybe someday we’ll determine they’re just further evolved.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your insights @kevink808. We want to assure you that Impulse is not a work of fiction—in fact, the voices you hear are those of the actual individuals recounting their real-life testimonies. Impulse is not intended to be a comprehensive portrayal of ADHD, as we recognise that the condition manifests in many ways across individuals. We are transparent about the fact that these particular stories represent extreme cases, as outlined in the synopsis. 

We interviewed over a hundred people, including those with lived experience, neuroscientists, psychiatrists, and campaigners who played an active role in shaping our editorial approach. We felt it was important to focus on people whose lives had been significantly affected by the condition. Whilst many manage their ADHD well with the support of those around them, often without structure, a patient teacher, or an insightful mentor, and in particular socio-economic circumstances, ADHD can have terrible outcomes. In fact, it is estimated that one in four prisoners in Britain are believed to have ADHD, and "untreated ADHD accounts for at least an estimated £11.7 million annually in the CJS" (https://www.adhdfoundation.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/Takeda_ADHD-in-the-CJS-Roundtable-Report_Final.pdf). Errol (one of the characters in Impulse) later found that he could manage this stimulation need through performance on stage; however, there is a resolution to each of the stories. We’re glad to hear that your own experience has been less dramatic or challenging, and we fully agree that many people with ADHD find environments where they can thrive, and yes, probably are the brains we will need for the future! 

We would love to hear more about your own experience. Please feel free to reach out to us at hello@impulse-xr.io.

reviews.review_description:
I find the experience incredibly boring. Besides, it doesn't look like the teaser.
The graphics aren't great, the stories... I've just disconnected from them. There was some good idea with the mixed reality dolls, but I don't think it's been developed well either.
I don't understand people who like it. Haven't they seen anything else in mixed reality? It makes me seriously think about not buying anything similar again without being able to see what the content is about, and look how much I like mixed reality, I buy everything there is... and I'm developing for VR. On top of that, I pay for it, when there are great free experiences.

Nothing to do with Goliath, which I also have and is very good.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback—we’re glad to hear you enjoyed Goliath. We appreciate your honesty and understand that experiences like Impulse can resonate differently for everyone. The Playing with Reality collection is designed to spark meaningful conversations around mental health, with MR playing a key role in the storytelling. We’d love to hear more about how the graphics and mixed reality elements didn’t meet your expectations, especially since we’ve received positive feedback in that area. Please feel free to reach out to us at hello@impulse-xr.io.

reviews.review_description: I'm deeply moved by this experience. The stories are so vivid, the scenes so alive and colorful and the music so beautiful...is really touching, mainly when I find parts of me in some of these stories.

developer_response.body: It’s great to know the stories and music struck a chord with you—make sure you check out Aaron Cupples' work (Impulse's Composer & Head of Sound)!

reviews.review_description: Both the visuals and the explanations were extremely interesting, each story was very heartfelt and in general this was one of the most impressive experiences I've had on VR. The use of mixed reality was mind boggling too. I absolutely recommend this to anyone.

I just wanted to say thank you for this experience.  It couldn't have arrived at a more perfect time.

Wandering around my playspace whilst experiencing stories of what I can only describe as amazing is definitely something everyone needs to experience!

developer_response.body: We’re glad to hear that this resonates with you! We wanted players to remain aware of their physical surroundings, emphasising that the room is still there and we’re not trying to completely disconnect you from it. The layered distractions reflect how our minds often operate—focused in one space while thinking about something else.

reviews.review_description: I was a huge fan of the first pwr game, so when I saw this i pre-ordered instantly, it was a beautiful and surreal experience don't listen to that one singular one star review this is great.
This was so moving and powerful. As someone who is going through the process of getting a diagnosis, it was so incredible to experience similar stories in this unique way. Mind. Blown. Thank you!

This is a great experience that takes you an an awesome visual journey! There are some negative reviews commenting on putting a negative light on people with ADHD, I disagree completely. It's very positive and engaging, taking you on their individual journeys with the challenges and extremes included. I'm pretty sure I have ADHD although it's undiagnosed, I can see how this might come across pretty personally for some people who ARE diagnosed. But I don't feel attacked, I just feel thankful that mine isn't extreme like these examples. All that aside, it's fascinating use of mixed reality with your living space unlike anything I've seen before. Goliath: Playing With Reality was excellent, and so is this. Worth every penny.

developer_response.body: Hi @GameListPlus Thanks for being with us since Goliath—we really appreciate your support! We’re also glad that Impulse resonated with you in such a positive way. The way you put it so eloquently mirrors our own belief that ADHD shows up differently for everyone, and Impulse isn’t meant to be a full picture of the condition. It shares four specific stories—Errol, Leanne, Omar, and Tara—who generously opened up about their journeys, highlighting both the challenges and the impact ADHD can have, especially when there’s a lack of understanding. Thanks again for your thoughtful feedback—it really means a lot!

reviews.review_description:
As someone with ADHD this has really been like the story of my life.  It might help people who now refuse to see me understand just what I’ve had to go through. All of my family are in that group.
Great job developers. You need to make it clearer it’s about ADHD.
Thank you.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your personal connection to Impulse @SecondsOut ! It's important that Impulse provides a truthful perspective on the way in which ADHD can impact one’s life and encourage public understanding of the complexities of ADHD. We hope the piece helps foster greater understanding within your circles.

reviews.review_description:
One of my favourite VR experiences was Goliath, so looked forward to this. 
While I was a little confused by the interactive element at first (take note: this uses the full space in 360°). Once I looked behind me again, it all made sense and we were away.
I won't give anything away about the content, but it's genuinely excellently done, immersive and at times (deliberately) disorientating.
If you loved Goliath, you'll love this. If you don't know Goliath, go get it now. Both deliver media in a way that only XR could.

developer_response.body: Hi @ wryly.elph, stoked to hear Goliath is one of your favourites! Always a pleasure hearing from fans who’ve been with us from the very start. With Impulse, there may be moments of distraction both within the experience and in your play space, and we ask players to stay open-minded as these elements overlay to become part of an embodied journey. Indeed, don't forget to look all around as sometimes the answer is right behind you!
